game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: Players operate an ice cream sundae shop, taking customer orders at the Order Station, then meticulously preparing them through the Build, Blend, and Top Stations, aiming for precision in pouring, blending, and topping placement. The game's core system revolves around customer satisfaction, where speed and accuracy in fulfilling orders directly impact the tips earned and customer patience meters.
game_info.description: Papa's Freezeria To Go! is a time management game where you run an ice cream shop. Take customer orders and craft perfect sundaes by moving through different stations. Pour ice cream, add mix-ins, blend to the right consistency, and finish with toppings. Satisfy customers by matching their orders precisely to earn tips and build your reputation. Balance speed and accuracy as customers become impatient while waiting!
game_info.controls:
Left Arrow: Move left between stations
Right Arrow: Move right between stations
Up Arrow: Move up in the current station menu
Down Arrow: Move down in the current station menu
Space: Select/Confirm action at current station
Z: Cancel current action
Shift: Rush/Speed up certain actions
Enter: Start game (at start screen)
Esc: Pause/Unpause game
R: Restart game (at game over screen)
game_info.automated_testing:
<TEST_1>
<test_description>Testing basic gameplay functionality and error handling by using sticky keys to randomly navigate between stations and perform actions.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Randomly switch between movement keys and action buttons to navigate stations, select items, and attempt to complete orders.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>Game runs without errors through multiple cycles of customer orders, demonstrating core mechanics function properly under random inputs.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_1>
<TEST_2>
<test_description>Testing the win condition by efficiently completing multiple customer orders with high accuracy.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Systematically move through stations in optimal order, selecting correct ingredients and performing actions with proper timing to maximize customer satisfaction.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>Successfully complete the required number of orders with high satisfaction ratings to reach the win condition and trigger the GAME_OVER_WIN state.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_2>
<TEST_3>
<test_description>Testing order accuracy impact on customer satisfaction by deliberately creating imperfect sundaes.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Complete orders with varying levels of accuracy - some perfect, some with minor errors, and some with major deviations from the requested order.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>Customer satisfaction meters respond appropriately to order accuracy, with perfect orders yielding high satisfaction and tips, while poor orders result in decreased satisfaction.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_3>
<TEST_4>
<test_description>Testing time pressure mechanics by deliberately taking excessive time at certain stations.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Process orders normally but introduce deliberate delays at specific stations to observe how waiting time affects customer patience and satisfaction.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>Customer patience meters decrease during delays, potentially leading to unhappy customers and reduced tips when orders take too long to complete.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_4>
<TEST_5>
<test_description>Testing station transition logic by rapidly switching between stations in unusual patterns.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Move quickly between stations in non-standard sequences, attempting to skip steps or return to previous stations out of order.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>Game properly enforces the required station sequence for order completion, preventing progression when attempting to skip essential steps.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_5>
